checks.output_summary: tsc -b clean, vite build produced dist/ successfully (39 modules transformed, no errors).

3 test files, 14 tests, all passed. src/App.test.tsx (6 tests, incl. updated /graph row), src/routes/QueryView.test.tsx (3 tests, pre-existing, unaffected), src/routes/GraphView.test.tsx (5 tests, new: neighbor-count render, deep-link auto-fetch, empty-state, error-state, empty-path guard).

matrix_coverage.AC-1: covered by GraphView.test.tsx 'renders the expected number of neighbor nodes after selecting a file' and 'auto-fetches via a ?path= deep link' tests
matrix_coverage.AC-2: covered by the same test's screen.findAllByTestId('graph-neighbor') length assertion (3), plus the empty-state test asserting length 0
matrix_coverage.NFR-1: no new npm dependency added (package.json diff is empty); fetch-mocking/state-machine/data-testid conventions mirror QueryView.test.tsx/QueryView.tsx
matrix_coverage.NFR-2: covered by 'renders an error message when the /graph call fails' test

deviation_from_plan: plan.md did not anticipate needing to touch ui/src/App.test.tsx. Running the full test suite (self-consistency step) surfaced a pre-existing failure: App.test.tsx's /graph router-test row still asserted the old on-mount 'graph-status' testid, which GraphView no longer renders now that graph fetching is user-triggered (same situation 6.1.2 hit and fixed for /query in commit 71cb1bb). Fixed by updating that one row to 'graph-form', following the exact precedent already documented in App.test.tsx's own comment for the /query row.
self_consistency_only_disclaimer: This is internal build/test sanity only, run by the implementation agent. It is NOT the independent adversarial verification required by invariant I4 -- that is /cdr:verify's job.
